description: A flexible line made for tying, lacing, or carrying modest tension is 'nira'. It commonly consists of twisted, braided, or sheathed strands. 'sima' (thread) handles finer textile work, while 'suro' (rope) is made for more substantial pulling or support.
articulatory_notes: Dental nasal 'n' begins behind the upper teeth and opens into stressed high-front 'i'. A tap or brief trill releases the final open 'a'.
usage_notes: The boundary between cord and rope follows construction and use as well as thickness. A bounded length can take optional object classifier 'themo'.

examples.translation: I tie the container with cord.

semantic_domains.creation: a flexible line made for tying, lacing, and light support
semantic_domains.physical: several strands held together as a flexible line
